title: Lithospermum subsetosum
taxonomy: Lithospermum subsetosum is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family comprises flowering plants, many of which are herbs, shrubs, or small trees. The genus Lithospermum includes about 100 species, most of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Lithospermum subsetosum is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, with a variety of species found in these regions. The classification of Lithospermum subsetosum places it within the order Lamiales, which includes various flowering plants with five-merous flowers.

intro: Lithospermum subsetosum is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae), native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum subsetosum include iridoid glycosides, such as lithospermic acid and its derivatives, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithospermum subsetosum is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
